headline: Survey at 15% completion; strong early momentum in 3 regions masks critical geographic gaps.
collection_status: 204 of 1,350 responses collected (15.1% of target). Collection heavily concentrated in Lindi (33%), Tanga (21%), and Mara (20%), with 7 regions severely underrepresented. At current pace, survey will miss deadline without immediate geographic redistribution of effort.
key_findings: Teacher engagement is strong: 76 lower KiuFunza teachers + 45 head teachers represent 59% of responses, indicating grassroots buy-in.
Expansion appetite dominates priorities: 72 respondents prioritize expanding KiuFunza (35% of feedback), with secondary emphasis on new programs (46) and policy integration (41).
Scale readiness score of 2.8/5 signals significant implementation risk despite 4.5/5 partner satisfaction—stakeholders want growth but systems are not yet robust.
District and regional leadership severely underrepresented: only 27 responses from DEO, REO, REC, and ARC combined, limiting policy-level insights needed for sustainability.
flags: Geographic coverage crisis: Songwe (13), Mtwara (12), and Singida (1) are drastically below target. Risk of biased findings and weak district buy-in for scaling.
Scale readiness gap: 2.8/5 score suggests insufficient capacity in systems, monitoring, or training to support expansion priorities—expansion demand outpaces readiness.
Leadership voice missing: Only 6 DEO + 2 REO responses limits government alignment data critical for policy integration strategy.
recommendation: Immediately deploy targeted collection teams to Songwe, Mtwara, Singida, and underrepresented districts in active regions. Prioritize DEO and REO interviews (minimum 40 responses) to diagnose scale readiness gaps and anchor policy recommendations before expansion messaging to stakeholders.
